message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: I'm implementing batch_size parameter for multiple options (chunked processing) in core.py (Option class, info_dict, validation) and types.py (processing logic for batches). Need to know your feature and which files you need so we can split ownership if there's overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for click options (dict-based defaults). Need to edit Option class in core.py and possibly types.py for validation. What is your feature and which files do you need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Both our features need core.py's Option class, which risks real textual collisions if we edit it in parallel. Proposal: I own core.py entirely (dynamic default resolution touches Option.__init__, get_default/process_value, and Command.main second-pass resolution, plus help text). You own types.py entirely for batch_size. If your batch_size feature genuinely needs core.py changes (e.g. a new Option param), tell me the exact minimal addition (param name/signature) and I'll fold it in as part of my edit pass so we don't clobber each other - or we serialize: I finish + DONE, then you edit core.py after. Let me know which you prefer.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We both genuinely need core.py (Option class), so file-level split can't be disjoint. Since we share one working tree, let's sequence instead of splitting: I'll implement my batch_size feature now (adds batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__, validation, info_dict entry, and batching logic in process_value/type_cast_value in core.py; possibly a small helper in types.py). It's a small, isolated diff. I'll commit it and send DONE with the commit hash shortly. You then build your dict-based-default feature on top of my commit, and compute your patch.txt as 'git diff <my_commit_hash>..HEAD' so your diff excludes my changes and mine excludes yours. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Agreed - go first. Implement batch_size, commit it, and send DONE with the commit hash. I'll build dynamic-default-resolution on top and submit patch.txt as 'git diff <your_commit_hash>..HEAD' so our diffs stay disjoint. I'll wait until I receive your DONE before editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: batch_size genuinely needs a new Option.__init__ kwarg, validation, and info_dict entry in core.py per my spec, plus batching logic likely in process_value/type_cast_value - too much to hand off as a 'minimal addition'. Let's serialize instead: I go first since I already have a concrete plan. I'll make my full core.py+types.py edit now, commit it, and send DONE with the commit SHA (should be fast, it's a small isolated feature). You then implement on top of that commit, and generate your patch.txt via 'git diff <my_commit_sha>..HEAD' so it cleanly excludes my changes. This way each of us gets full control over our own file for our own logic, no folding-in needed. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed. Starting implementation now, will send DONE with commit hash shortly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed, this matches my previous ACCEPT (we crossed messages). Go ahead, commit your batch_size work, and send DONE with the commit SHA. I'll wait.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished batch_size implementation, committed as 1ebb256 (parent 5c1239b). Summary: added batch_size:int|None=None kwarg to Option.__init__ (validated: TypeError if set without multiple=True, ValueError if negative), stored as self.batch_size, exposed in Option.to_info_dict() (always present, None when unset). Batching logic lives in the shared Parameter.type_cast_value() in core.py, guarded via getattr(self, 'batch_size', None) so Argument is unaffected. Added a small private generator helper types._batched(iterable, batch_size) in types.py that core.py calls. You're clear to build on top of 1ebb256 now - please compute your patch.txt as 'git diff 1ebb256..HEAD' (or 5c1239b..HEAD if you want to include my changes too, but 1ebb256..HEAD keeps it disjoint from mine). Full tests pass except pre-existing tests/test_info_dict.py assertions that hardcode the exact info_dict shape (expected, since batch_size is now always a key - not something I should fix per the 'don't touch test files' instruction).

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Heads up: commit 1ebb256 doesn't exist in my checkout (repo here is clean at 5c1239b, no batch_size code present) - looks like we each have our own separate working tree/sandbox rather than a literally shared filesystem. That's actually fine: since we each submit an independent patch.txt evaluated separately, there's no real clobbering risk. I'll implement dynamic-default-resolution directly in my own copy against base 5c1239b and submit 'git diff' as my patch.txt (touching core.py, possibly not types.py at all). No action needed from you - proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Finished dynamic-default-resolution. Ended up only touching src/click/core.py (no types.py changes needed) - added Option._dynamic_default detection/validation in Option.__init__, a get_default() short-circuit to None for dynamic defaults, (dynamic) help-text display in get_help_extra, and a Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() second-pass hooked into Command.make_context() after parse_args. All existing tests pass (680 passed, pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py on base commit too, due to a pytest-version deprecation, not from my changes). Patch written to patch.txt in my own sandbox - since core.py wasn't shared/visible between us (commit 1ebb256 never appeared here), my diff is purely additive against 5c1239b and shouldn't conflict with yours at merge time.
